title: Malty French Wild Garlic Terrine
description: A twist on the classic terrine, this dish combines the earthy flavors of wild garlic with a malty crispness, perfect for a lazy Sunday.
instructions: Caramelize the chopped onion in a saucepan over low heat, stirring occasionally, for 30 minutes.
Chop the wild garlic leaves and set aside.
In a separate saucepan, whisk the eggs and milk together, then add the chopped onion and whisk until combined.
Butter a 9x13 inch baking dish and pour the egg mixture into it.
Sprinkle the wild garlic leaves, grainy mustard, and chopped fresh thyme over the egg mixture.
In a separate bowl, mix the all-purpose flour and malt flour together, then add the mixture to the egg mixture and stir until just combined.
Pour the egg mixture into the prepared baking dish and smooth the top.
Bake in a preheated oven at 375°F (190°C) for 25-30 minutes, or until the edges are golden brown.
Remove from the oven and let cool to room temperature.
Cover the dish with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours.
To unmold, dip the dish in an ice bath and then invert onto a serving plate.
Serve chilled and garnish with additional wild garlic leaves and chopped fresh thyme, if desired.
